title: Piquant Smoky Greek Chicken Kebabs
description: A crowd-pleasing Greek dish featuring smoky olive oil and double-fried chicken, this recipe is egg-free and friendly for all, with a bitter and shaggy texture that is sure to delight.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together smoky olive oil, lemon juice, garlic powder, onion powder, salt, black pepper, and paprika.
Add the chicken to the bowl and toss to coat with the marinade.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Heat about 1/2 inch of olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Double-fry the chicken kebabs in the skillet for about 5 minutes on each side, until browned and crispy.
Transfer the kebabs to a baking sheet and bake in the preheated oven for 15-20 minutes, or until cooked through.
Serve the kebabs hot, garnished with fresh parsley or dill if desired.
